objective: Define a Qlib-independent typed factor-expression AST and operator registry, then prove the TrueAlpha compiler is the only path that can supply an expression to pinned Qlib 0.9.7.
claim_ceiling: E0 safe typed-expression contract, deterministic compiler, execution identity, and isolated Qlib 0.9.7 parity over the frozen synthetic corpus; no approved product semantics, real factor, provider, registration, replay, persistence, stable handoff, or release-readiness claim

requirements: queued S8 registration is merged to main
the typed-expression corpus path and SHA-256 are committed in a preparation PR
the implementation PR pins its exact current main base SHA
the implementation reuses the S7 workspace-excluded Qlib runtime and does not change the root lock
an independent reviewer is assigned before E0 acceptance
the Gate 0-frozen shared contracts export remains unchanged; a later export requires a fresh integration lease and candidate rebind
issue #189 contains the exact manifest path and SHA-256

dependencies.artifact: accepted executable Core Strategy definition contract
accepted pinned Qlib runtime and selection-adapter boundary
approved matrix-compatible factor semantics and product expressions
user-approved Qlib architecture selection and version-pinned adapter ownership

required_strata: canonical feature, finite numeric, and registered operator-call nodes
builtin-only arithmetic, reference, and rolling operators with exact arity, argument kinds, null behavior, and lookback rules
deterministic AST, registry, expression, compiled-field, and combined execution identities
independent typed-AST evaluator parity with pinned Qlib on a tiny dimensionless matrix
arithmetic null propagation, Ref null preservation, Mean skip-null behavior, and derived maximum lookback
raw string, callable, module path, kwargs, attribute, indexing, import, and punctuation injection rejection
unknown feature/operator, duplicate ID/symbol, invalid arity/type, non-finite numeric, excessive depth/node/lookback rejection
input reordering, ambient Decimal context, root-environment import, and root-lock isolation

release_activation.reason: S8 defines provisional safe expression plumbing only; #59/#21 semantic approval, real factor candidates, #71 verification, and #26 replay remain mandatory

negative_controls: raw Qlib field string, Python callable, arbitrary module path, kwargs, or engine configuration input
feature name with punctuation, quoting, attribute access, indexing, comprehension, import, or mutable coordinate
unknown feature binding or operator registry entry
duplicate operator ID or Qlib symbol and stale registry identity
invalid operator arity or argument kind
NaN, infinity, excessive AST depth, node count, or maximum lookback
expression, feature binding, registry, null policy, or lookback identity drift
attempted Decimal monetary, graph, PIT vintage, confidence, lineage, provider, runner, or release semantics

allowed_evidence_readers: issue #26 Qlib provider and replay integration batch

invalidation: AST node vocabulary, operator semantics, feature binding, compiler grammar, identity partition, null behavior, lookback, corpus, or output change creates a new batch revision
shared contracts export requires an exact active integration lease before implementation
a Qlib runtime or lock change requires a new execution binding and rerun but must not silently change expression semantics
product semantics, real factor data, provider execution, or materialization does not raise this provisional evidence ceiling
rollback: Revert the isolated S8 contract, compiler, fixture, tests, and lease together; no accepted factor registry, provider, runner, or release may reference this provisional expression.
